data.title: ServiceNow gains as Oppenheimer ups target on 'positive checks' ahead of Q3
data.description:
• ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) rose 1% in premarket trading on Thursday as Oppenheimer raised its price target on the cloud software giant, citing "positive" checks going into the company's third-quarter results.
  • "Based on our preview mosaic, ServiceNow executed well and had a strong 3Q," analyst Brian Schwartz wrote in a research note. "However, next week's earnings set-up looks less favorable, with multiples already predicting good 3Q results and a materially successful outcome for the business over the FTM. In addition, there is a high 2025 subscription revenue growth expectation. In our view, management's 4Q guidance is unlikely to raise growth estimates and may underwhelm investors."
  • Schwartz, who reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $1,020 from $825, said the pending third-quarter release "will likely reaffirm investors’ positive opinion" on the company and its momentum.
  • "Further, the company is also seen as a winner in the generative AI era and is executing well in the current operating environment," Schwartz wrote. "We remain bullish on ServiceNow over the long term."
  • ServiceNow is slated to report third-quarter results after the close of trading on Oct. 23. A consensus of analysts expect the company will earn $3.45 per share on $2.75B in revenue.

data.title: Old Second Bancorp raises dividend by 20% to $0.06
data.description:
• Old Second Bancorp (NASDAQ:OSBC) declares $0.06/share quarterly dividend, 20% increase from prior dividend of $0.05.
  • Forward yield 1.49%
  • Payable Nov. 4; for shareholders of record Oct. 25; ex-div Oct. 25.
  • See OSBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Nasdaq futures rise after robust results from TSMC, strong retail sales
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Thursday, with Nasdaq futures leading the pack, after strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and robust retail sales, helped revive investor sentiments.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.6%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.9% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.01%.

Initial jobless claims matched and fell to 241K in the past week, while U.S. retail sales climbed more than expected in September. The Philly Fed outlook came in at +10.3 in October.

Wall Street on Wednesday ended higher, rebounding from a rout in chip stocks in the previous session. An ongoing rotation into small-caps and other sectors buoyed markets, while financials surged on a post-earnings pop in Morgan Stanley (MS).

"Markets put in a solid performance yesterday, with investors remaining upbeat as they digested several earnings results and another modest decline in oil prices," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) shares were up 7% in premarket trade, after it beat both its Q3 bottom and top line as it continued to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom, and helped traders renew their confidence in tech stocks.

The September industrial production and the Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

"Manufacturing output (on the official data) has risen slightly this year. Sentiment (ISM, Philly Fed) has pointed to a near continuous contraction for the past two years. It is enough to make one question whether sentiment lives in the real world," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Badger Meter GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.07, revenue of $208.4M misses by $3.75M
data.description:

data.title: Philly Fed outlook +10.3 in October
data.description:
• October Philly Fed Business Outlook: +10.3 vs. +4.2 consensus, +1.7 prior.
  • The diffusion index for future general activity +36.7 vs. prior +15.8.
  • The employment index -2.2 vs. +10.7.
  • The future capital expenditures index was 23.50 vs. prior +25.
  • New Orders +14.2 vs. prior -1.5.

More on economic indicators

  • U.S. retail sales climb more than expected in September
  • Initial jobless claims, matching consensus, fall to 241K in past week

data.title: ASML removed from top picks at Cantor Fitzgerald after weak guidance
data.description:
• ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) was in the spotlight again on Thursday after Cantor Fitzgerald removed the chip equipment maker from its top picks, following its weak guidance earlier this week.
  • Shares rose 0.5% in premarket trading but have dropped 17% over the past five trading sessions.
  • ASML's shares are likely in the "penalty box for a bit" until investors can regain confidence for growth into 2026, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C. J. Muse said. As such, he believes shares will be range-bound between €600-700.
  • Muse kept his Overweight rating, however, as shares are "near trough-like levels" and there is a path over the next 6 to 12 months that could see the stock return to roughly €750.

data.title: General Motors in talks to move Asian manufacturing to Mexico
data.description:
In an effort to reduce imports into the country from Asia and increase domestic manufacturing, Mexico’s deputy head of industry and trade, Vidal Llerenas, is in talks with several industrial giants including General Motors (NYSE:GM), Foxconn, and Intel (INTC) to begin manufacturing locally.

GM (GM) and Foxconn are expected to announce plans to swap out products manufactured in Asia for production in Mexico later this month, Llerenas recently said to Reuters, adding that the Mexican government is also currently in talks with Stellantis (STLA) and DHL. Intel (INTC) already plans to substitute 12% of the products it ships to Mexico to begin local manufacturing.

Of the total imported vehicles into Mexico, 40% come from Asia, according to industry data with General Motors (GM) the number one importer at 65% made in China, followed by Sweden’s Volvo (OTCPK:VLVLY) at 54%, and Ford (F) with 24% of all imported vehicles into Mexico made in China.

As recently as 2022, calling it "the most convenient thing to do," GM (GM) moved manufacturing of the Chevy Onix from Mexico to China, exporting it back to Mexico rather than import the Brazilian made Onix to Mexico. Production of other models destined for Central American markets also moved from Brazil to China, rather than being manufactured in Mexico.

data.title: Total Energy Services files to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Total Energy Services (TSX:TOT:CA) said it has filed a notice with the Toronto Stock Exchange to conduct a normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the normal course issuer bid, the firm may purchase up to 1.91M shares.

data.title: ECB cuts key interest rates by 25 bps
data.description:
The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year, after policymakers flagged reduced inflation risks.

Markets now closely watching President Christine Lagarde's speech to gain more insights on the central bank's strategy.

 

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (HEWG), (DAX), (FLFR), (FLGR), (FLIY)

Currency: (EUR:USD)
